lever: typed Int64 IndexLabels storage: lazy Arc<Vec<i64>> backing in fp-index IndexLabels (mirrors the int64_unit_range lazy precedent) + typed i64 label gather in fp-frame take_rows_by_positions_unchecked
scenario: filter_bool 100000 rows x 10 cols, 200 iters (perf_profile example, release-perf)

pure_op_per_iter_ms.speedup: ~1.25x op-level (index-side cost ~eliminated; column side already typed)

mechanism: per-row 32B IndexLabel enum clone -> i64 gather loop; rename_index deep Vec<IndexLabel> clone -> O(1) Arc clone; element-walking Vec<IndexLabel> drop (String drop glue) -> single Vec<i64> free; labels materialize lazily only when labels() is asked for
heuristic: uncached typed view computed only when positions.len()*4 >= len (avoids O(n) scan for narrow takes like head(5) on huge frames); cached view always used

isomorphism.ordering_preserved: yes - gather order is positions order, identical to enum-clone loop
isomorphism.label_values: identical - lazy materialization maps i64 -> IndexLabel::Int64, same values byte-for-byte

test_gates.fp-index + fp-frame --lib --tests: 2817 passed; 3 failed = pre-existing on clean main (arr72 Debug-golden drift, filed br-frankenpandas-5gns6)
test_gates.fp-io + fp-join --lib --tests: green (578 + 101 passed; one global-csv-parse-cache test flaked once under parallel ordering, passes 5/5 isolated-suite and 2/2 combined reruns with lever; passes on main full-suite 2/2)
test_gates.concat_bench example: pre-existing compile breakage on main (private column_order()), unrelated
